Title: Manipulating the Light Systemic Signal HY5 Greatly Improve Fruit Quality in Tomato

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HY5-mediated activation of LIN5 promotes sucrose conversion and increases glucose and fructose accumulation during fruit ripening.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ripening fruit; elevated around breaker stage
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit at 43 DPA and ripening stages; induced by HY5, red light, and blue light
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of WT vs hy5 fruits at 40 DPA; accession NA
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; CWINV activity assay; hy5 mutant and HY5 overexpression analysis; grafting; light treatment
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit bagging; nighttime dim red light; nighttime dim blue light
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for improving sweetness when combined with HY5 manipulation or light management.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LIN5 transcript is reduced in hy5 and increased in OE-HY5 fruits. 2. HY5 directly binds the LIN5 promoter and activates its transcription. 3. LIN5 contributes to enhanced sucrose cleavage and hexose accumulation during ripening.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Studied mainly as a direct downstream target of HY5 rather than an independent causal gene.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native LIN5 promoter analyzed in Y1H/EMSA/dual-LUC

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HY5-mediated activation of LIN6 promotes sucrose conversion and hexose accumulation during fruit ripening.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Ripening fruit; elevated around breaker stage
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit at 43 DPA and ripening stages; induced by HY5, red light, and blue light
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of WT vs hy5 fruits at 40 DPA; accession NA
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; CWINV activity assay; hy5 mutant and HY5 overexpression analysis; grafting; light treatment
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit bagging; nighttime dim red light; nighttime dim blue light
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for fruit sweetness improvement in combination with HY5-based engineering or supplemental lighting.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LIN6 expression is positively associated with HY5 abundance. 2. HY5 directly binds the LIN6 promoter and activates transcription. 3. Increased LIN6 likely contributes to higher CWINV activity and hexose accumulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Studied as a direct HY5 target.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native LIN6 promoter analyzed in Y1H/EMSA/dual-LUC

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HY5 positively regulates VI transcription, promoting sucrose conversion during ripening.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit at 43 DPA and 55 DPA; induced by HY5 and supplemental light
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of WT vs hy5 fruits at 40 DPA; accession NA
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; mutant and overexpression analysis; grafting; light treatment
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit bagging; nighttime red light; nighttime blue light
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential sugar-improvement target coordinated by HY5.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VI transcript decreases in hy5 and increases in OE-HY5 fruit. 2. HY5 directly binds the VI promoter and activates transcription. 3. VI likely contributes to elevated glucose and fructose accumulation in ripening fruit.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Downstream HY5 target in sugar metabolism.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native VI promoter analyzed in Y1H/EMSA/dual-LUC

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HY5 activates SS1 expression and promotes sucrose metabolism during fruit ripening.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit at 43 DPA and ripening stages; induced by HY5 and supplemental light
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of WT vs hy5 fruits at 40 DPA; accession NA
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; mutant and overexpression analysis; grafting; light treatment
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nighttime red/blue light; fruit ripening stage
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target to improve sugar conversion in ripening fruit.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SS1 is downregulated in hy5 and upregulated in OE-HY5. 2. HY5 directly binds the SS1 promoter and activates transcription. 3. SS1 participates in the HY5-controlled sugar metabolism network.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native SS1 promoter analyzed in Y1H/EMSA/dual-LUC

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HY5 activates SS7 expression and promotes sucrose metabolism during fruit ripening.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit at 43 DPA and ripening stages; induced by HY5 and supplemental light
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of WT vs hy5 fruits at 40 DPA; accession NA
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; mutant and overexpression analysis; grafting; light treatment
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nighttime red/blue light; fruit ripening stage
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for improving sugar metabolism together with HY5-based strategies.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SS7 transcript is positively regulated by HY5. 2. HY5 directly binds the SS7 promoter and activates expression. 3. SS7 contributes to HY5-mediated sugar accumulation in fruit.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Figure 5K model text mentions SS6, likely a typographical inconsistency; core results repeatedly validate SS7.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native SS7 promoter analyzed in Y1H/EMSA/dual-LUC

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two molecules of geranylgeranyl pyrophosphate to form phytoene.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies direct precursor for lycopene and beta-carotene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nutritional quality, provitamin A source for beta-carotene, antioxidant/pigment contribution to fruit quality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression of PSY1 is activated by HY5, promoting carotenoid accumulation and ripening-associated fruit coloration.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Peaks around breaker stage (40-43 DPA) during ripening
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit at 43 DPA and 55 DPA; reduced in hy5 and bagging, increased in OE-HY5 and supplemental red/blue light
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of WT vs hy5 fruits at 40 DPA; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR hy5 mutant; HY5 overexpression; RT-qPCR; Y1H; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; grafting; metabolite profiling; light treatment
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit bagging; nighttime dim red light; nighttime dim blue light
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for carotenoid biofortification, especially in combination with HY5 activation or light supplementation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PSY1 expression peaks near breaker stage together with HY5 accumulation. 2. PSY1 transcript decreases in hy5 and increases in OE-HY5 fruits. 3. HY5 directly binds the PSY1 promoter and activates transcription, elevating lycopene and beta-carotene.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Validated as a direct HY5 target central to the fruit carotenoid response.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Desaturates phytoene toward zeta-carotene during carotenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms pathway intermediate required for lycopene and beta-carotene accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step not definitively established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nutritional quality, antioxidant pigmentation, provitamin A contribution via beta-carotene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HY5 directly activates PDS transcription, promoting carotenoid biosynthesis and fruit coloration.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Peaks around breaker stage during ripening
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit at 43 DPA and 55 DPA; decreased in hy5 and bagged fruits, increased in OE-HY5 and under red/blue light
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of WT vs hy5 fruits at 40 DPA; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR hy5 mutant; HY5 overexpression; RT-qPCR; Y1H; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; grafting; metabolite profiling; light treatment
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit bagging; nighttime red light; nighttime blue light
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful carotenoid-pathway target for improving fruit nutritional value via HY5-centered engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PDS transcript is positively associated with HY5 abundance. 2. HY5 directly binds the PDS promoter and activates expression. 3. Elevated PDS correlates with enhanced lycopene and beta-carotene accumulation during ripening.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct HY5 target in carotenoid biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: bZIP transcription factor; mobile light-signaling regulator
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HY5/bZIP transcription factor recognizing Z-box, G-box/C-box, and ACGT-containing cis-elements
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus; mobile protein detected translocating from leaves to fruits
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Protein accumulation peaks at breaker stage (~40 DPA); transcript transiently increased at 40 DPA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Accumulation in fruits peaks at breaker stage; induced in leaves and fruits by nighttime red or blue light; lower in bagged fruits but still peaks at ripening onset
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit bagging; grafting; nighttime supplemental red light (660 nm); nighttime supplemental blue light (430 nm)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improve tomato fruit nutritional quality, pigmentation, sweetness, and total soluble solids
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HY5 positively promotes tomato fruit ripening by increasing carotenoid biosynthesis and sucrose conversion, thereby enhancing lycopene, beta-carotene, glucose, and fructose accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: carotenoid biosynthesis; sucrose conversion; sugar metabolism; systemic light-regulated fruit ripening
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master positive regulator and systemic mobile light signal controlling both carotenoid synthesis and sugar metabolism during tomato fruit ripening.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PSY1 and PDS; activates VI, SS1, SS7, LIN5, LIN6, and LIN8
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PSY1; PDS; VI; SS1; SS7; LIN5; LIN6
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation via promoter binding to HY5 cis-elements; systemic protein translocation from leaves to fruits
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Light; nighttime supplemental red light; nighttime supplemental blue light; COP1 antagonism implied
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: COP1 (antagonistic factor, mentioned genetically/physiologically not direct interaction here)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; overexpression; immunoblotting; grafting with HA-tagged HY5; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; Y1H; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; metabolite profiling; CWINV activity assay; supplemental light treatment
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of WT and hy5 fruits at 40 DPA; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth or yield penalty reported in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for tomato quality improvement through overexpression, light management, or mobilization-based strategies; low-dose red/blue LED supplementation is a practical deployment route.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HY5 protein peaks at breaker stage, coincident with carotenoid accumulation and sugar metabolism gene induction. 2. hy5 knockout delays ripening and reduces lycopene, beta-carotene, glucose, fructose, and CWINV activity, whereas HY5 overexpression has the opposite effect. 3. HY5 directly binds and activates promoters of PSY1, PDS, VI, SS1, SS7, LIN5, and LIN6. 4. HY5 protein moves from leaves to fruits and acts as a systemic light signal. 5. Nighttime dim red and especially blue light enhance HY5 accumulation and improve fruit quality in a HY5-dependent manner.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although many downstream genes were measured, HY5 is the primary experimentally manipulated causal gene in the Results. Figure 5K mentions SS6, but the validated gene throughout the Results is SS7.
